title: Travel to Transform: What Kind of Traveller Are You?
description: Discover your travel archetype in minutes.

elements.name: q1
elements.title: When you think about travel in your life, which statement resonates most? (Multiple answers allowed)

choices.text: A) Travel is my reward after working hard. I'm always finding ways to earn my next escape
B) Travel is how I learn about myself and the world around me
C) Travel is a strategic tool for creating better life opportunities and breaking free from societal limits
D) Travel is where I create multiple bases in different countries to create positive impacts as part of my global lifestyle

elements.name: q2
elements.title: When faced with travel challenges (delays, cultural misunderstandings, plans changing), you most likely:

choices.text: A) Feel frustrated and stressed – these things ruin my carefully planned trip
B) See it as part of the exploration and try to learn from the experience
C) View it as data for optimising future travels and building my longer-term global journey
D) Flow with it naturally without expectation, knowing deeply that every experience serves my purpose perfectly

elements.name: q3
elements.title: When looking at your recent travels, which motivation or focus describe them best?

choices.text: A) Rest, relaxation, and escaping from daily routine or work stress
B) Learning about other cultures, languages, history, and expanding my worldview
C) Creating new (business) opportunities, strategic networking, building global communities, or lifestyle design
D) Genuine service, deep cultural integration, co-creating positive impacts alongside the local communities I visited

elements.name: q4
elements.title: How do you currently fund your travel experiences?

choices.text: A) I save up from my job or business, then allocate a travel budget for vacations/holidays
B) I travel as affordably as possible, sometimes working remotely, doing short-term gigs, or finding creative ways to extend my trips
C) I have intentionally built flexible income streams to support my travels, or I'm actively working on doing so
D) My lifestyle, network, and mission are globally integrated and financially sustainable. Travel is part of my daily life and income

elements.name: q5
elements.title: When it comes to planning travel, what best describes your approach?

choices.text: A) I plan thoroughly in advance and feel most comfortable with a structured itinerary
B) I create a loose plan but leave lots of room for spontaneity and unexpected adventures
C) I plan with specific life and business goals in mind, with each trip serving a deliberate purpose in my bigger vision
D) Planning feels natural and effortless. My travels are guided by both intuition and purpose, flowing seamlessly with my life mission

elements.name: q6
elements.title: When you return home after a trip, what do you normally experience?

choices.text: A) Post trip blues – I miss being away and feel like I'm back to the daily grind
B) Inspired but slightly unsettled – I want to keep the experience going but don't quite know how
C) Clarity and momentum – I have lessons to apply and I'm intentionally connecting travel insights to my life and career
D) Seamlessness – there's very little sense of "returning home" because travel and home are integrated parts of my life

elements.name: q7
elements.title: How do you typically connect with others while travelling?

choices.text: A) I mostly travel with family or friends I already know, or I stick to guided group tours
B) I love meeting new people but often feel a gap – most travellers I meet don't have the same depth of curiosity as me
C) I actively create connections that align with my personal and professional mission, attending events or retreats with like-minded people
D) Deep, meaningful connections across different cultures come naturally. I am drawn to serve, collaborate, and co-create positive change with others easily

elements.name: q8
elements.title: Which best describes your current relationship with the idea of "freedom"?

choices.text: A) Freedom means having time off from work to travel, even if it's just once or twice a year
B) I'm actively seeking more freedom – I want to travel more and break free from the conventional model of work and life
C) I'm building systems and structures that give me growing freedom – financially, geographically, and in time
D) I embody freedom. It's not something I seek – I express it through how I live, lead, and contribute to the world

elements.name: q9
elements.title: What does "home" mean to you today?

choices.text: A) Home is where I live, where my comfort zone is, and where I return after every trip
B) Home is becoming less of a fixed place – I'm drawn to the idea of feeling at home in multiple cities or countries
C) I've intentionally created a base that gives me both roots and the freedom to explore. Home is more of a strategic anchor
D) Home is within me. I feel at ease and at home wherever I am in the world

elements.name: q10
elements.title: When you think about the impact you want to make through travel, which resonates most?

choices.text: A) I mostly think about the personal enjoyment. The biggest impact is on my own mood, health, or energy
B) I want my travels to make me a better, wiser, and more open-minded person who can contribute more at home
C) My travels are part of a bigger vision to build something meaningful – I want to create real value for others and myself
D) My life and travels are an expression of positive impact. I'm called to be part of a global movement for conscious change

elements.name: q11
elements.title: Which of these travel experiences would excite you the most right now?

choices.text: A) A well-planned luxury beach resort holiday or a guided cultural tour with all logistics taken care of
B) A solo adventure to an off-the-beaten-path destination with lots of room for spontaneity and cultural discovery
C) An immersive international retreat or mastermind focused on personal growth and building meaningful global connections
D) Leading or co-creating a transformative global experience that changes lives – including yours and the participants

elements.name: q12
elements.title: How aligned do you feel between your travel life and your everyday life?

choices.text: A) They feel very separated. Travel is the exciting escape from my everyday life
B) I'm starting to sense a gap and want them to feel more integrated, but I'm not sure how
C) I've been intentionally bridging them, and they're starting to feel more connected and purposeful
D) There's no separation. My travel and everyday life are one holistic, joyful expression of who I am

elements.name: q13
elements.title: Finally, what do you most want to gain from your travels going forward? (Multiple answers allowed)

choices.text: A) More rest, joy, and time to recharge away from the demands of work and responsibilities
B) Deeper self-awareness, growth, and a clearer sense of who I am and what I want
C) Greater freedom, sustainable income, meaningful purpose, and an elevated global network
D) To lead, inspire, and serve at a global level – and to keep expanding into my highest potential on this earth
